verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Main blocker: Surfline consistency is lully.

conversationalSummary.text: Tomorrow is probably one to skip. Cerro at After work (18:30 start) looks like the least-bad option, but not really something worth chasing. The lack of push looks like the main issue and could easily make it messy. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam first, but unless it looks surprisingly clean, I'd save the drive.

best.bestWind: ideal SSW, usable S/SW/SSE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Cerro looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 11 kJ, 9 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
best.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for after work (18:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size
Cerro may feel too soft without more push
